Annotation Type AuditLog

@Target(METHOD) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Documented public @interface AuditLog
Records a structured audit-trail entry every time the annotated method is invoked.

The entry is built by AuditLogAspect and published as a Spring AuditEvent. The default handler (SigNozAuditHandler) logs it via SLF4J with the AUDIT marker so it appears in SigNoz logs with full trace correlation.

Fields captured automatically:

  • traceId / spanId — from the active OpenTelemetry span
  • actor — from SecurityContextHolder (if Spring Security present)
  • timestamp — UTC instant of the call
  • outcome — SUCCESS or FAILURE (on exception)

Usage: 

  @AuditLog(action = "USER_LOGIN", resourceType = "User", captureArgs = false)
  public AuthResult login(String username, String password) { ... }

  @AuditLog(action = "TRANSFER_FUNDS", resourceType = "Account", captureArgs = true)
  public Transfer transfer(String fromId, String toId, BigDecimal amount) { ... }

  • Required Element Summary

    Required Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Required Element
    Description
    String
    action
    The action name recorded in the audit entry (e.g.

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    boolean
    captureArgs
    Whether to capture and log method arguments in the audit entry.
    boolean
    captureResult
    Whether to capture the return value in the audit entry.
    String
    resourceId
    SpEL expression evaluated against the method arguments to extract the resource ID.
    String
    resourceType
    The type of resource being acted upon (e.g.

  • Element Details

    • action

      String action
      The action name recorded in the audit entry (e.g. "USER_LOGIN", "DELETE_RECORD"). Should be an uppercase, underscore-separated verb.

    • resourceType

      String resourceType
      The type of resource being acted upon (e.g. "User", "Order").
      Default:
      ""

    • resourceId

      String resourceId
      SpEL expression evaluated against the method arguments to extract the resource ID. Example: resourceId = "#order.id" when the method takes an Order argument. Leave empty to omit the resource ID.
      Default:
      ""

    • captureArgs

      boolean captureArgs
      Whether to capture and log method arguments in the audit entry. Disable for methods that accept sensitive parameters (passwords, tokens). Defaults to true.
      Default:
      true

    • captureResult

      boolean captureResult
      Whether to capture the return value in the audit entry. Defaults to false to avoid logging large response objects.
      Default:
      false
